I WILL DECOMPOSE THE SYSTEM PROMPT INTO FOUR INDEPENDENT MODULES, EACH ADDRESSING A DISTINCT ASPECT OF THE TASK. THE MODULES WILL GUIDE THE USER THROUGH THE PROCESS OF BREAKING DOWN THE ORIGINAL INSTRUCTION, APPLYING CONSTRAINTS, AND GENERATING A COMPLETE, COMPLIANT RESPONSE. THE FINAL RESPONSE WILL BE IN ENGLISH, UNDER 36 SENTENCES, AND START AND END WITH THE SAME WORD. NO MARKDOWN IS ALLOWED. WORDS WITH ALL CAPITAL LETTERS WILL APPEAR NO MORE THAN THREE TIMES. THE RESPONSE WILL BE SELF-CONTAINED AND LOGICAL.

MODULE 1: IDENTIFY THE CORE TASK. THE TASK IS TO DETERMINE IF MOVIE (1) AND MOVIE (2) REFER TO THE SAME ENTITY. THIS INVOLVES ANALYZING THE DATA IN BOTH TABLES AND COMPARING KEY ELEMENTS SUCH AS YEAR, DIRECTOR, TITLE, WRITERS, AND ACTORS.

MODULE 2: ANALYZE MOVIE (1). THE DATA SHOWS MOVIE (1) WAS RELEASED IN 2015, DIRECTED BY PETER DELUISE, AND FEATURED R.L. STINE'S MONSTERVILLE: THE CABINET OF SOULS. THE WRITERS INCLUDE DAN ANGEL AND BILLY BROWN, AND THE ACTORS ARE DOVE CAMERON, KATHERINE McNAMARA, AND RYAN MCGARTHAN.

MODULE 3: ANALYZE MOVIE (2). THE DATA SHOWS MOVIE (2) WAS RELEASED IN 2014, DIRECTED BY PETER JACKSON, AND FEATURED THE HOBBIT: THE BATTLE OF THE FIVE ARMIES. THE WRITERS INCLUDE J.R.R. TOLKIEAN, PETER JACKSON, AND PHILIPP A BOYENS. THE ACTORS ARE MARTIN FREEMAN, IAN McKELLAN, AND RICHARD ARMITAGE.

MODULE 4: COMPARISON AND ERROR PREVENTION. THE YEAR, DIRECTOR, AND TITLE ARE DIFFERENT BETWEEN MOVIE (1) AND MOVIE (2). THE WRITERS AND ACTORS ARE ALSO DIFFERENT. THERE IS NO OVERLAP IN THE DATA. THE RESPONSE WILL BE "NO" AS THE TWO MOVIES REFER TO DIFFERENT ENTITIES.
